name: research
description: v2.5.0 - Agent-driven R&D protocol with prior-work grounding, independent result-analysis quality gates, assumption audit, theoretical mode, and paper-grade report/claim structure.

interface.shortDescription: Agent-driven R&D protocol with prior-work grounding, claim discipline, and v2.5.0 result-analysis support
interface.longDescription: v2.5.0 - A protocol skill for agent-driven R&D across the three OECD Frascati Manual 2015 categories: basic_research, applied_research, and experimental_development. Includes the research lifecycle (observation discovery → hypothesis synthesis → intervention → prior-work grounding → plan → execution → result analysis → research review → claim → decision). Plan modes are exploratory, confirmatory, milestone, and theoretical; theoretical mode is for derivational work with axioms, definitions, and limiting-case checks rather than empirical evaluation. Research ideation is substrate-driven: de-anchored raw seeds become candidates only after they cite substrate ids, name generation operators, state changed premises, pass an anti-vacuity gate, record candidate-keyed blind-spot entries, and record evaluator feedback or a named missing-evaluator constraint. Prior work has two roles: material for observations and grounding after candidates exist. Approach transition criteria connect results to NEXT_STEP, REFINE, ADJACENT, PARK, and CLOSE. v2.5.0 adds the dedicated research-result-analysis skill with a plan-path-only subagent prompt before Research review; the analyzer reconstructs evidence independently, records context_missing, stages Observation → Interpretation → Claim-readiness, applies artifact-faithful / arithmetically checked / claim-fit checked / depth-calibrated / reviewable quality gates, and must not translate claim-readiness into GO/NO-GO or iteration decisions. v2.3.0 added references/assumption_audit.md, which surfaces background assumptions of the reference model being challenged, distinct from the anchor audit on imported prior work, using a load-bearing audit with downstream-check, blind-spot catalog entries tied to mechanism failure paths and claim-scope effects, manual reference-class forecasting, and constraint-naming for un-evaluable hypotheses. v2.4.0 adds references/iterative_ideation.md (F v2): an AlphaEvolve-style evolutionary ideation protocol with mandatory real shell / command-line execution and explicitly forbidden self-simulation, for applied_research and experimental_development plans whose domain has an existing executable evaluator. Separate-context analysis means any host-provided fresh agent context, not a specific Task tool. v2.4.0 also makes report_format.md paper-grade with required Theory/Related Work/Ablation/Discussion/References sections, a Figure-as-argument convention, theoretical report guidance for derivational work, and a Statistical reporting minimum (effect size, CI, n, variance, significance) for numeric evidence. `check_idea_portfolio.py` verifies the Idea portfolio substrate/operator/anti-vacuity/blind-spot/evaluator-feedback contract. Methods reproducibility is enforced; experiment-level replicability is the agent's discretion, while provenance and claim-to-artifact checks are treated as audit/evidence-integrity controls.
defaultPrompt: Design an R&D plan for this investigation
Help me decide what to do after this experimental result
Write a human-readable report from this plan and runs
